about.metaDesc: AlfyChat is developed by AlfyCore, a French non-profit association dedicated to private and secure communication.
about.heading: A messenger built with values.
about.intro: AlfyChat is built by {org}, a non-profit association. Our mission: offer private, secure and sovereign communication to everyone.
about.missionP1: We believe communicating privately is a fundamental right, not a privilege. Against tech giants who monetise your conversations, AlfyCore offers a free, transparent and France-hosted alternative.
about.missionP2: AlfyChat is not funded by advertising. It lives thanks to donations, volunteers and community support. Every line of code is written to protect — not to exploit.
about.contactBody: For any question about the AlfyCore association, reach us at {email}. For security reports: {security}.

values.title: Privacy first
values.desc: 3-level encryption, data hosted in France, zero ad profiling. Your privacy is not a product.

values.title: Community & sharing
values.desc: AlfyChat was born from a community and stays open to all. Code is open source, community feedback drives our priorities.

values.title: Digital sovereignty
values.desc: 100% France-hosted, native GDPR compliance, alternatives to US giants — for a more independent internet.

values.title: Performance & reliability
values.desc: Microservice architecture, HD calls, low latency. Professional quality with non-profit values.

values.title: Inclusion & accessibility
values.desc: Free for everyone, no ads, no data resale. Available on every platform: web, mobile, desktop.

values.title: Transparency
values.desc: Our accounts, decisions and roadmap are public. AlfyCore commits to full transparency with its users.

milestones.label: AlfyCore founded
milestones.desc: Non-profit association founded by tech and privacy enthusiasts.

milestones.label: First prototype
milestones.desc: MVP launch: direct messaging and community servers.

milestones.label: Open beta
milestones.desc: Opening to the community, HD calls, bots, mobile app.

milestones.label: AlfyChat v2
milestones.desc: Complete UI redesign, performance, Go infrastructure.

faq.metaDesc: AlfyChat frequently asked questions. Find answers about the messenger.
faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to your most common questions

items.a: AlfyChat is a French, open-source, GDPR-compliant instant messenger. It offers 3-level encryption, HD calls, community servers and custom bots.
AlfyChat is built and managed by AlfyCore, a French non-profit association. Our mission is to offer a privacy-respecting alternative.
Yes, AlfyChat is completely free. The project is funded by donations and community support.
You must be at least 13 to create an AlfyChat account, per French law.
Level 1: encryption in transit (TLS). Level 2: server-side AES-256-GCM encryption. Level 3: ephemeral keys — not even AlfyCore can read your messages; keys are destroyed after reading.
In level 1, technically yes (moderation). In level 2, only if legally required. In level 3, it is impossible: we have no access to keys.
All data is hosted in France, on sovereign servers. No transfer outside the EU.

items.q: Can I edit or delete a message?
items.a: Yes. At encryption level 3, deletion is automatic after reading.
Images are capped at 10 MB and auto-optimised to WebP. Larger-file support is planned.
Yes! You can react with emojis on any message.
Calls use WebRTC for peer-to-peer, high-quality communication. Voice, video and screen sharing are available.
Yes, WebRTC natively encrypts audio and video streams via SRTP.

items.q: How do I create a server?
items.a: Click the "+" in the sidebar to create your server. You can then add channels, manage roles and invite members.

items.q: Can I host my own server?
items.a: Self-hosted P2P servers are in development. You will be able to host your own servers like on TeamSpeak.

items.q: Can I build my own bot?
items.a: Yes, AlfyChat provides an API to build your own bots. See the developer docs to learn more.

items.q: How do I export my data?
items.a: Go to Settings → Privacy → Export my data. You will receive a JSON file with all your information.

items.q: How do I delete my account?
items.a: Go to Settings → Privacy → Delete my account. Deletion is irreversible and effective within 30 days.
The username cannot be changed after signup. You can, however, change your display name at any time.

jobs.metaDesc: Join AlfyCore and help build tomorrow's private messenger.
jobs.heading: Let's build tomorrow's messenger together.
jobs.intro: AlfyCore is a non-profit association. Our roles are volunteer missions — join us to contribute to an impactful open-source project.
jobs.unsolicitedBody: Can't find a role matching your profile but want to contribute? Send us a message — we are always looking for motivated people.

whyJoin.title: Real impact
whyJoin.desc: Your code is used by real people who need to communicate privately.

whyJoin.title: Modern stack
whyJoin.desc: TypeScript, Next.js 15, Go, Flutter, React Native, Tailwind v4.

whyJoin.title: 100% open source
whyJoin.desc: Everything you build is visible and reusable by the community.

openings.title: Full-Stack TypeScript developer
openings.description: Contribute to the backend (NestJS microservices) and the React/Next.js frontend. Code reviews and architecture.

openings.title: Go developer — Infrastructure
openings.description: Work on the Go server (server-node-go): REST API, WebSocket, performance and scalability.

openings.title: Security engineer
openings.description: Audit the E2E encryption implementation, find vulnerabilities, write reports and propose fixes.

openings.title: UI/UX designer
openings.description: Design and refine AlfyChat's interface: components, mobile (Flutter & React Native), design system.

openings.title: Community manager & comms
openings.description: Animate the community, publish news, write articles and handle user feedback.

openings.title: Technical writer
openings.description: Document APIs, write install guides, improve wiki and README pages.

newsroom.metaDesc: All the latest news, updates and announcements from AlfyChat and AlfyCore.
newsroom.heading: AlfyChat news feed.
newsroom.intro: Updates, announcements and milestones from the AlfyChat and AlfyCore project.
newsroom.stayTunedBody: To follow AlfyChat news, join our official server or follow us on our socials. You can also subscribe to our technical newsletter at {email}.

entries.title: AlfyChat v2.0 — Full redesign
entries.summary: Fully redesigned UI, Go microservice infrastructure, Tailwind v4, new design system based on HeroUI. Performance and accessibility improved on every platform.
items: New design system — components redesigned with HeroUI React
Go server (server-node-go): latency reduced by 40%
React Native mobile app (alfychat-rn)
Dark / light / auto theme system
Graphic charter redesign — Krona One as main typography

entries.title: Level-3 encryption — Ephemeral keys generalised
entries.summary: Level-3 encryption is now available for all DMs. Keys are generated client-side and destroyed after reading. Not even AlfyCore can read your messages.
items: AES-256-GCM + ephemeral keys for all DMs
Visual indicator of the active encryption level
Independent security audit published — report available
Automatic message deletion after 30 / 90 days (configurable)

entries.title: HD Calls — Better video & voice
entries.summary: New call engine with AI noise reduction, video quality up to 1080p30, and persistent voice rooms on servers.
items: 1080p30 video quality in group calls
Built-in AI noise reduction (client-side)
Persistent voice rooms on community servers
Screen share with system audio
New floating call interface

entries.title: Public beta — Welcome!
entries.summary: After two years of development, AlfyChat opens its doors to the community. Create your account, join a server and give us feedback.
items: 5,000 accounts created in the first 48 hours
Community servers available from sign-up
Bots and integrations API open to developers
Flutter app available on Android and iOS
Beta-tester program with priority access to new features

entries.title: Switch to microservice architecture
entries.summary: AlfyChat migrates from a Node.js monolith to a distributed architecture: gateway, messages, users, bots, calls, friends — each service independent.
items: 7 independent microservices deployed via Docker Compose
Nginx gateway with rate-limiting and JWT
MySQL + Redis for presence cache and sessions
Deployment time cut by 3

entries.title: Custom emojis & reactions
entries.summary: Add your own emojis to your servers and react to messages with standard or custom emojis.
items: Custom emoji upload per server (admin required)
Quick reactions on messages
Built-in emoji picker with search
Animated emoji (GIF) support

rgpd.metaDesc: AlfyChat's GDPR compliance. Exercise your rights over your personal data.
rgpd.heading: GDPR — Your rights
rgpd.subtitle: General Data Protection Regulation
rgpd.intro: AlfyChat is committed to respecting the GDPR (EU 2016/679). You hold rights on your personal data that you can exercise at any time.

sections.title: Right of access
sections.body: You have the right to know what data we hold about you. In your {settings}, you can view all your personal information.

sections.title: Right to portability
sections.body: You can export all your data in JSON format from your account settings. The export includes: profile, messages, friends, preferences and consents.

sections.title: Right to rectification
sections.body: You can modify your personal information at any time from your profile settings: display name, avatar, bio, email.

sections.title: Right to erasure
sections.body: You can request the complete and irreversible deletion of your account and all associated data.
bullets: Deletion is effective within 30 days max.
All data is erased: profile, messages, files, sessions.

sections.title: Right to object and restrict
sections.body: You can object to or restrict the processing of your data. Contact our DPO to exercise these rights.

sections.title: Data controller
sections.body: AlfyCore — Non-profit association — Email: contact@alfycore.org — DPO: dpo@alfycore.org

sections.title: Complaint
sections.body: If you believe your rights are not respected, you may submit a complaint to your national data-protection authority.

hero.subtitle: End-to-end encryption, French hosting, zero tracking. Brought to you by AlfyCore — a non-profit association, free and open source forever.

features.heading: Everything you need.
features.subtitle: Built for privacy, performance and simplicity.

e2ee.name: E2EE Encryption
e2ee.description: ECDH P-256 + AES-256-GCM. Your keys never leave your device.

global.name: Global presence
global.description: Hosted in France, accessible worldwide. GDPR-native, zero tracking.

gateway.name: Gateway & Microservices
gateway.description: Distributed architecture: a central gateway orchestrates 7+ independent microservices.

bots.name: API & Bots
bots.description: Open SDK. Build your own bots and integrations.

promise.body: Zero-knowledge encryption is not a premium feature at AlfyChat. It’s the bare minimum. For everyone, from day one.

architecture.body: Node.js microservices, MySQL, Redis, Socket.IO, WebRTC. Each component is open source and can be audited, forked or self-hosted independently.

preview.body: Direct messages, servers with roles and permissions, voice rooms, file sharing. All end-to-end encrypted.

messages.text: Hey! Have you seen the new release? 🎉
And the WebRTC calls are rock solid 🔥

security.body: The server only stores opaque ciphertexts. All encryption happens client-side. AlfyCore technically cannot read anything — not messages, not calls, not files.

selfHosting.body: Host your own AlfyChat node on your VPS or PC. Your messages and files stay stored locally, without ever passing through our servers.

link.title: Link your server
link.desc: Grab your token from the AlfyChat interface.

start.title: Start
start.desc: alfychat-server start — your messages stay with you.

download.body: Native apps for all your devices. Always free, always open source.

association.body: AlfyCore is a non-profit association, founded in France. No shareholders, no ads, no tracking. Just the community.

finalCta.body: No ads. No tracking. Free and open source, forever.

footer.tagline: Open source encrypted messenger. Brought to you by AlfyCore, a non-profit association.
